page: Amazon Web Services c8a.xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The server is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, which provides 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance type, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8a.xlarge demonstrates top-tier single-core performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU test, Geekbench single-core score of 2817.0, and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression rates all rank in the top 10%. Text processing and text generation for small LLM models also achieve top-tier speeds. Conversely, multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores, falls into the average category. Memory bandwidth tests, static web serving throughput, and database operations also align with average tier performance.

The instance offers high resource density for single-threaded tasks, making it highly cost-efficient for workloads that do not require massive parallelization or large memory footprints. However, the 2.0 GB of RAM per core and the lack of local NVMe storage represent a tradeoff for memory-intensive or high-I/O database applications. The c8a.xlarge is best suited for compute-bound applications, single-threaded processing tasks, web serving, software compilation, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, offering a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core performance, including high single-threaded compression and text processing speeds, while multi-core metrics and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. This hardware profile provides qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy, single-threaded tasks but presents a tradeoff in memory capacity. It is well-suited for software compilation, web serving, and lightweight inference workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance delivering top-tier single-core performance and DDR5 memory for dedicated, high-frequency processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs.
Equipped with 8.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory.
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance.
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor.
Lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, prioritizing raw compute efficiency.
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and lightweight inference.
